paper_title: Grain-size, Foraminiferal, and Cluster Analysis in Coastal Sediment Studies

methods.description: Grain-size analysis was conducted on surface samples to determine the distribution and characteristics of sediment particles, which is crucial for understanding sediment transport and depositional environments.

steps.description: Organics and carbonates were removed using 30% hydrogen peroxide and 10% hydrochloric acid and dried at 50°C for 72 h.
steps.searchable_summary: remove organics and carbonates from samples using chemical treatment and drying

steps.description: Grain-size analysis was conducted using a Camsizer.
The average of three replicates was determined before converting grain-size values to the Wentworth-Phi Scale.
steps.searchable_summary: average replicates and convert to Wentworth-Phi Scale

steps.description: Grain-size values and abundances for each transect were interpolated and gridded using a Triangular Irregular Network (TIN) algorithm and plotted as Particle-Size Distribution (PSD) plots.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate and plot grain-size data using TIN algorithm

steps.description: Statistical parameters including mean, mode, standard deviation, skewness, kurtosis, and d10, d50, and d90 were used to characterize each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate statistical parameters for grain-size characterization

methods.description: Foraminiferal analysis was conducted to identify living versus dead individuals and to assess the total assemblage, which helps in understanding past environmental conditions and sediment transport.

steps.description: Surface sediment samples were stained with Rose Bengal and stored in buffered ethanol to identify living versus dead individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: stain and store sediment samples to differentiate living and dead foraminifera

steps.description: Sediment samples were washed over a 63 lm sieve and wet split to obtain counts of ca 300 specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and split sediment samples to count foraminifera

steps.description: The total number of foraminifera contained within 10 cm3 was calculated for each sample site.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total foraminifera per sample site

steps.description: Elevation or depth, total foraminifera, abundance of dead individuals, dominant species, and taphonomic character were plotted against distance from the shoreline.
steps.searchable_summary: plot foraminiferal data against shoreline distance

methods.description: Cluster analysis was used to objectively determine biofacies, taphofacies, lithofacies, and combination-facies, which were compared with observed geomorphic beach zones.

steps.description: Taxonomic, taphonomic, and grain-size values were standardized by converting to abundances and calculating Z-scores.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize data by converting to abundances and calculating Z-scores

steps.description: PAM cluster analysis was performed using the 'cluster' package in R to identify zonation within the coastal transect.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PAM cluster analysis using R

steps.description: Silhouette plots were generated to estimate sample classification and determine the number of clusters.
steps.searchable_summary: generate silhouette plots to determine cluster number

steps.description: Seven combinations of data were clustered using % abundances to determine spatial and regional variability.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster data combinations to assess variability
